findings.summary: generate_pipeline_report unpacks ReportExtras into six local variables used exactly once
evidence: Six consecutive assignments extract every field of extras into identically-named local variables (test_results, validation_results, performance_metrics, error_summary, output_statistics, project_name)
Each local is used exactly once — either passed directly to PipelineReport or used in the log_file side-effect block
Pattern reads as AI padding: x = extras.x if extras is not None else None repeated six times
findings.suggestion: Use extras directly (extras.test_results etc.) in the PipelineReport constructor, or let ReportExtras carry a to_dict()/to_report() method. Eliminate the intermediate locals entirely.

findings.summary: logging_utils.__all__ re-exports 30+ symbols from five submodules making the public surface non-obvious
evidence: __all__ lists 30 symbols spanning formatters (JSONFormatter, TemplateFormatter), spinner classes (Spinner, StreamingProgress), ETA functions (calculate_eta, calculate_eta_ema, calculate_eta_with_confidence), and constants (EMOJIS, USE_EMOJIS)
Callers that only need get_logger must load the import graph of all five submodules — the public surface is broader than any single caller needs
Sibling __init__.py files (validation/__init__.py, rendering/__init__.py) export only the symbols that belong to their domain, not everything from all sub-modules
findings.suggestion: Trim __all__ to the core logging API: get_logger, setup_logger, log_operation, log_timing, log_stage, log_substep, format_duration. Callers needing Spinner or ETA functions should import from logging_progress directly.

findings.summary: StagePerformanceTracker.end_stage() returns empty dict when not started; PerformanceMonitor.stop() raises BuildError for same precondition
evidence: PerformanceMonitor.stop(): raises BuildError('Monitor not started') when start_time is None
StagePerformanceTracker.end_stage(): logs debug then returns {} when start_time is None — same precondition, different strategy
Callers cannot distinguish 'stage never ran' from 'stage produced empty metrics' when receiving {}
findings.suggestion: Align both classes: either both raise on unstarted state, or both return a typed sentinel (e.g. None or a dedicated NotStarted dataclass). Silent empty dict is harder to debug than an explicit error.

findings.summary: logging_utils.py remains a fan-in hub imported by 134 modules, aggregating five submodules with no owned logic beyond get_logger/setup_logger
evidence: logging_utils imports from logging_formatters, logging_helpers, logging_constants, logging_progress (twice) and _optional_deps — it owns none of this logic
With 134 importers, a change to any of the five submodules triggers reloads through logging_utils, amplifying blast radius
The split was done for file size, not ownership — the module hierarchy doesn't reflect domain boundaries
findings.suggestion: Either collapse the five submodules back into logging_utils.py (cohesion is high, the split adds indirection without isolation) or move them into a logging/ subpackage with a clean __init__.py as the public API.

findings.summary: profile_memory_usage uses result_holder list as closure workaround and indexes metrics_history[-1] directly
evidence: result_holder = [] used as a mutable container to return a value from a nested thread function — a Python 2-era pattern unnecessary since Python 3 nonlocal is available
metrics_history[-1] accessed immediately after append without guard — if profiling fails before append, IndexError
CodeProfiler.monitor() has a we_started_tracing boolean flag to manage tracemalloc state — the flag and conditional stop could be replaced by a try/finally with tracemalloc.is_tracing()
findings.suggestion: Replace result_holder list with nonlocal or a threading.Event + shared variable. Guard metrics_history access. Simplify tracemalloc state management with is_tracing().
